task: I need to leave a clean note for the next person about a room issue, but I don’t want to leave out anything important. Can you help me with the wording and the order it should go in so it’s easy to pass along?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple order that makes the note easy to read and hard to miss anything important:

1. Room number
2. What the issue is
3. Where it’s coming from / what it’s affecting
4. What’s already been done
5. Whether the guest knows
6. Anything that needs follow-up or urgency

Plain note format you can use:

Room [number] — [brief issue]. Source appears to be [source]. Affects [carpet/outlet/furniture/wall/etc.]. [What has already been done]. Guest is [aware/not aware]. Needs follow-up by [next shift/front desk/manager].

Example:

Room 214 — Water leak under the sink. Source appears to be the cold water line. Affects cabinet base and nearby carpet. Maintenance was notified and towels placed to contain the water. Guest is aware. Needs follow-up by next shift.

If you want, I can also help you turn your exact room issue into a clean handoff note.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Simple and to the point, which is what I needed. The order makes sense too — room, problem, what it’s hitting, what was done, then who needs to know next. I can use that.
